metadata.summary: I WANT TO BUILD A COMPREHENSIVE WEBSITE FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF GEOGRAPHY AND REGIONAL PLANNING AT THE UNIVERSITY OF CAPE COAST: THESE ARE MY CONSDERATI...

turns.content: I WANT TO BUILD A COMPREHENSIVE WEBSITE FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF GEOGRAPHY AND REGIONAL PLANNING AT THE UNIVERSITY OF CAPE COAST: THESE ARE MY CONSDERATIONS: ABOUT  PAGE WHICH SHOULD HAVE BRIEF SUMMARY OF THE DEPARTMENT AND A CAROSOL OF PICTURES DISPLAYING NEXT AND VIDEOS. THERE SHOULD BE LEVELS 100 - 400 AND ALSO AWARDS SECTION AND YEARLY VALIDIDOTIRIAM SECTION. THE LEVELS SHOULD HAVE ALL THE COURSES THERE AND DESCRIPTION AND OTHERS I HAVE NOT MENTION. PROBABILY MONTHLY BLOCK AND OTHERS THIS IS MY SCHEMATIC TEMPLATE BUT I WANT YOU TO DO A RESEARCH AND SEE HOW WE CAN MAKE IT A VERY COMPREHENSIVE, FUN AND EDUCATED WEBSITES.  BUT LETS DISCUSS WHAT IS POSSIBLE AND WHAT TO BRING ON BOARD. YOU ARE AN EXPECT WEB DEVELOPER AND A PROFESSOR IN COMPUTER SCIENCE

turns.content:
Great question! Here's the honest breakdown:

## 🗄️ Database: Do We Need One?

### Option A: **Static Website (No Database)** — What I'm Currently Proposing

| Aspect | How It Works |
|---|---|
| **Courses** | Hardcoded in HTML/JS — data lives in the code |
| **Faculty profiles** | Hardcoded cards |
| **Valedictorians** | Hardcoded list |
| **Blog/News** | Static HTML articles |
| **Gallery** | Images stored in a `/images` folder |

✅ **Pros**: Free hosting (GitHub Pages/Netlify), zero maintenance, fast, no server needed  
❌ **Cons**: To update content, you must **edit the code** every time

---

### Option B: **Dynamic Website (With Database)** — When You'd Need One

A database becomes essential if you want:

| Feature | Why It Needs a Database |
|---|---|
| **Admin Panel** | Add/edit/delete courses, news, faculty **without touching code** |
| **Monthly Blog Posts** | Write and publish articles from a dashboard |
| **Valedictorian entries** | Add new winners each year via a form |
<truncated 1798 bytes>
